text.title: feature-flags-services-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-flags-services-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent forgets the OFF-branch test entirely, writing only the happy-path test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the gate: faion-sdd-executor-agent MUST require both branches tested before marking the task done.

failure-mode.description: Removal is harder than addition: agent deletes the flag check but leaves the OFF branch orphaned in the code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use piranha or a two-step review: first confirm the flag is at 100%, then delete both the check and the old branch in the same commit.

failure-mode.description: During incidents, agent flips the wrong flag because flag keys are ambiguous or similarly named.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require explicit flag key + dry-run output before any production flag flip. Human-in-loop for kill-switch operations.

failure-mode.description: Cross-service flag drift: same flag name, different semantics in service A vs service B.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: User sees broken state when service A treats the flag as enabled and service B treats it as disabled. Lock the registry and validate flag name uniqueness across services in CI.
